title: Richard Dixon
notes: Early member of the Dixon family, establishing a local presence that would shape Whittington across several generations.

title: Richard Dixon Jnr
notes: Continued the family’s local influence during a period of gradual parish development.

title: William Dixon
notes: Part of the Dixon succession, maintaining continuity in landholding and local standing.

title: Richard Dixon Jnr 2
notes: Represents the later phase of Dixon family influence, overlapping with the arrival of Dr Samuel Pegge.

title: Dr Samuel Pegge
notes: Rector of Whittington and noted antiquarian. His work preserved important local history and coincided with the later Dixon period.

title: Gilbert Dixon
notes: Associated with both local industry and administration, marking a transition from traditional landholding toward broader economic activity.

title: John Dixon
notes: A key late figure in the Dixon line, bridging the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries and overlapping with emerging new parish leadership.

title: William Beecher
notes: Held a short tenure during a transitional period in parish leadership.

title: Sherard Beecher
notes: Continued the Beecher clerical presence, guiding the parish into the early nineteenth century.

title: George Gordon (the Elder)
notes: Part of the clerical transition that followed the decline of the Dixon family’s dominance."

title: George Gordon Jnr
notes: A long-serving clerical figure whose tenure spans major social and industrial change in Whittington."

title: Frederick Swanwick
notes: Engineer associated with the railway age, contributing to the wider industrial transformation influencing Whittington.

title: Mary Swanwick
notes: Writer and reformer, reflecting the intellectual and cultural life of the Victorian and Edwardian periods.

title: New Parish Church Built
notes: Marks a major phase of Victorian expansion and investment in the parish."

title: Joseph Syddall
notes: Artist known for detailed works and local connections, contributing to Whittington’s cultural heritage.

title: George William Botham
notes: Late Victorian parish figure whose years fall within a particularly active period of local overlap."

title: Clement Clover Ward
notes: A short-serving parish figure during a transitional period before the 1895 church fire."

title: George Owen Holt
notes: Active in the years immediately leading up to the destruction of the church by fire."

title: John Tomlinson
notes: His years overlap the destruction of the old church and the rebuilding of its successor."

title: Church Destroyed By Fire
notes: A defining moment in Whittington’s history, leading to rapid rebuilding.

title: Maurice Deacon
notes: Managing Director of Sheepbridge Coal & Iron and Church Warden."

title: Present Church Built
notes: A symbol of resilience and renewal after the 1895 fire."

title: George Ford
notes: Led the parish into the twentieth century, consolidating the rebuilt church and modern community life.

title: Harry Brearley
notes: Metallurgist and pioneer of stainless steel, representing the area’s wider industrial legacy.

title: Plotting Of Revolution
notes: The 1688 Revolution provides an early historical anchor point for the wider story of the area."
